description_long: This object consists of a group of six wooden storage crates, each with a rectangular or cubic shape and constructed from vertical wooden planks. The crates are arranged in a loosely stacked and clustered formation, with some boxes placed atop others, creating a sense of depth and layering. The wood grain is visible on all surfaces, giving the crates a natural, unfinished appearance typical of shipping or storage containers. The edges and corners are reinforced with additional wooden framing, and the tops of the crates are flat, suggesting they can be stacked securely. There are no visible handles, locks, or labeling on any of the crates, and the surfaces are uniformly light tan in color. These crates are commonly found in urban environments such as warehouses, garages, or back alleys, where they serve as storage or transport containers.
description: A group of six light tan wooden storage crates with visible wood grain, arranged in a loosely stacked cluster. The crates have flat tops, reinforced edges, and no visible handles or locks.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the front and top faces of all six crates, highlighting their arrangement and the flat, plank-textured surfaces.
description_view_1: View 1 presents a side angle, emphasizing the height of the tallest crate and the staggered stacking of the boxes.
description_view_2: View 2 focuses on the rear and side faces, showing the alignment of the crates and the uniformity of their construction.
description_view_3: View 3 displays the opposite side, revealing the flat tops and the way the crates are grouped together.
